question.content: For the cast-iron I-section beam with unequal top and bottom flange widths, use the beam geometry and loading given in the accompanying image. The second moment of area about the centroidal z-axis is \(I_z = 235 \times 10^6\,\text{mm}^4\), with \(y_1=119\,\text{mm}\) and \(y_2=181\,\text{mm}\). The allowable tensile stress is \([\sigma_t]=40\,\text{MPa}\), and the allowable compressive stress is \([\sigma_c]=120\,\text{MPa}\). Determine the allowable distributed load \([q]\), keeping two decimal places. Answer format: a single numerical value; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Identify the governing mode first. Since [sigma_t] = 40 MPa is much smaller than [sigma_c] = 120 MPa, tension controls. Because y2 = 181 mm > y1 = 119 mm, the tensile critical edge is the bottom edge in positive bending and the top edge in negative bending.
step_1.key_point: Tension-controlled edges: positive moment -> y2, negative moment -> y1.

step_3.content: Use only tensile allowable moments: M_allow(+) = [sigma_t]*Iz/y2 = (40x10^6 x 235x10^-6)/0.181 = 51.93 kN*m, and M_allow(-) = [sigma_t]*Iz/y1 = (40x10^6 x 235x10^-6)/0.119 = 79.00 kN*m.
step_3.key_point: Tensile allowable moments are 51.93 kN*m (positive) and 79.00 kN*m (negative).

step_4.content: From the beam moment diagram, the peak moments are M_max(+) = 6.4q and |M_max(-)| = 8q. Match each peak to its tensile allowable value.

step_5.content: Solve the two bounds: q <= 51.93/6.4 = 8.11 and q <= 79.00/8 = 9.87. Take the smaller admissible value.

step_6.content: Therefore, the allowable distributed load is [q] = 8.11.
